title: Repeating forms use OC's non-standard XLSForm structure
description: Repeating forms use OC's non-standard XLSForm structure: data fields wrapped in a begin group / end group block (relevant-gated on the YN first-entry flag), followed by a trailer of begin repeat <form_id> + phantom end group (no matching begin_group) + end repeat. The end group and end repeat rows MUST have a BLANK name. Do NOT include a top-level SUBJID row on repeating forms. The first-entry YN gate uses relevant: ${REPKEY_ID}=1.
rationale: OC4 reads has_repeating_group metadata and bind__oc_itemgroup to drive its repeat semantics — NOT the XLSForm structural begin_repeat/end_repeat tags alone. The phantom end-group at the end of the trailer is required for OC's parser to correctly close the group context; without it, the form upload silently fails to activate. Blank name on end rows: pyxform looks for a matching begin_group/begin_repeat by name and rejects the form if it finds a name with no preceding open.

flag.message: Form ${form.form_id} is repeating. Verify: (1) data fields wrapped in begin group / end group, relevant-gated on YN first-entry flag (e.g. ${REPKEY_ID}=1); (2) trailer is begin repeat <form_id> + phantom end group (no matching begin_group) + end repeat; (3) end group and end repeat rows have BLANK name; (4) no top-level SUBJID row.

effect.soft: Emit the four-row trailer at the end of the survey: begin repeat <form_id>, phantom end group (no matching begin_group, blank name), end repeat (blank name). The wrapping begin group at the start uses relevant=${REPKEY_ID}=1 where REPKEY_ID is the local calculated display of the repeat key. Do not include a top-level SUBJID row — OC uses its built-in subject context for repeating forms.

history.note: Imported from prompts.py RULE OC-8 during Phase B.1a. Mechanical enforcement of the four-row trailer shape (blank-name end rows, phantom end-group placement) lives in build_preview/sanitize.py and skills/edc-builder/scripts/validate_form.py today. Phase C may migrate that validation into a structured DSL operator (e.g. 'match_trailer_pattern') and remove the duplicate enforcement code. The flag + soft directives carry the shape guidance to Claude and review_flags in the interim. Kind revised from `structured` to `hybrid` during B.1a translation because the four-row trailer shape (begin-repeat, phantom end-group, blank names, no SUBJID row) isn't expressible in the current DSL; mechanical enforcement lives in build_preview/sanitize.py and skills/edc-builder/scripts/validate_form.py. Soft directive carries the shape guidance.
